rationale: AC10 RED-on-revert lens; R8 finding-2 fix verified at 997e2a9e. (1) claimsLifecyclePackage now delegates to validate.GlobMatch(pattern, "internal/lifecycle/orphans.go") — production's **-aware matcher, no hand-rolled prefix. GlobMatch("internal/**", probe) matches (segs internal + trailing ** → true), so a broader second-domain glob IS now counted as a claimant. (2) I proved the broader-glob subtest is TRULY RED by mutating claimsLifecyclePackage back to the round-1 strings.HasPrefix heuristic in a throwaway worktree: broader_internal_star_star FAILED (only [workflow] detected — exactly R8's gap), and narrower_sibling ALSO failed (prefix wrongly claims internal/lifecycleX). Production GlobMatch fixes both; all 4 subtests + real-manifest test PASS at HEAD. (3) TestLifecycleOwnershipExactlyOneWorkflowClaimant passes against real manifests (workflow sole claimant). (4) No import cycle: go list -deps ./internal/validate shows 0 lifecycle deps; go build ./... green, go vet clean. Diff confined to 5 files. No still-missed overlapping glob, no non-discriminating subtest, no cycle.

findings.note: claimsLifecyclePackage uses production validate.GlobMatch against real probe path internal/lifecycle/orphans.go; broader internal/** overlap now detected (round-1 R8 gap closed).
Mutation to prior prefix heuristic makes broader_internal_star_star and narrower_sibling subtests FAIL; new matcher makes all pass. Subtests genuinely discriminate; not rubber-stamps.
TestLifecycleOwnershipExactlyOneWorkflowClaimant passes at HEAD; workflow is the sole real claimant.
internal/validate has 0 lifecycle deps; go build ./... + go vet ./internal/lifecycle green.
